meta.purpose: Encode how teams turn motion into reusable product design systems with tokens, primitives, and shared language.
meta.load_condition: Load when building a motion design system, tokenizing animation values, creating shared motion primitives, or scaling motion across a product team.

axioms.one_sentence: Define motion as design tokens (duration scales, easing curves, distance scales) before coding any animation.
axioms.full_statement: Define motion as design tokens (duration scales, easing curves, distance scales) before coding any animation.
axioms.why: Without tokens, every animation is a one-off decision. Tokens make motion repeatable, reviewable, and consistent across an entire product — the same way color and spacing tokens work.

axioms.one_sentence: Build reusable motion primitives (fade-in, slide-up, scale-reveal) instead of inline animation on every component.
axioms.full_statement: Build reusable motion primitives (fade-in, slide-up, scale-reveal) instead of inline animation on every component.
axioms.why: Primitives create a shared library of tested, accessible, performant behaviors. Components consume primitives; they don't redefine motion from scratch.

axioms.one_sentence: Name motion primitives by their semantic purpose, not their visual effect.
axioms.full_statement: Name motion primitives by their semantic purpose, not their visual effect.
axioms.why: A 'modal-enter' communicates intent across designers and engineers. A 'scale-fade-400ms-ease-out' communicates only implementation. Semantics enable the team to discuss motion without knowing CSS.

axioms.one_sentence: Motion system documentation must include copy-pasteable code snippets and live previews.
axioms.full_statement: Motion system documentation must include copy-pasteable code snippets and live previews.
axioms.why: Designers cannot use a motion system they can't see. Engineers cannot adopt a motion system they can't copy. Static screenshots of animation are useless documentation.

ontology.one_sentence: A design token specifically for animation: duration scales (75ms, 150ms, 300ms), easing curves (entrance, exit, attention), and distance scales (near, medium, far).
ontology.essence: A design token specifically for animation: duration scales (75ms, 150ms, 300ms), easing curves (entrance, exit, attention), and distance scales (near, medium, far).
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'motion token' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: A reusable, named, and documented animation behavior with defined enter/exit states, timing, easing, and reduced-motion fallback.
ontology.essence: A reusable, named, and documented animation behavior with defined enter/exit states, timing, easing, and reduced-motion fallback.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'motion primitive' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: A named motion primitive tied to a UI state change (e.g., 'modal-enter', 'notification-dismiss', 'page-reveal') rather than a visual description.
ontology.essence: A named motion primitive tied to a UI state change (e.g., 'modal-enter', 'notification-dismiss', 'page-reveal') rather than a visual description.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'semantic transition' or describes this scenario.

ontology.one_sentence: The full set of rules connecting tokens, primitives, and semantic transitions into a coherent brand motion language.
ontology.essence: The full set of rules connecting tokens, primitives, and semantic transitions into a coherent brand motion language.
ontology.boundary: This is not the same as a generic implementation detail or unrelated concept.
ontology.trigger_signal: User input mentions 'motion grammar' or describes this scenario.

frameworks.name: Motion token ladder
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'Motion token ladder' pattern.
steps: audit existing animations across the product
group by semantic purpose (entrance, exit, attention, state-change)
extract common duration, easing, and distance patterns
define token scales (3-5 values per token type)

frameworks.name: Adoption framework
frameworks.when_to_use: Apply when the task matches the 'Adoption framework' pattern.
steps: build primitives with live previews (Storybook or similar)
document each primitive with copy-pasteable code
add motion lint rules for CI (ban inline animation outside primitives)
track adoption: % of components using primitives vs. one-offs

core_structure.from: existing animations, brand guidelines, accessibility requirements, framework constraints
core_structure.to: tokenization fit, semantic clarity, reusability, accessibility compliance, adoption feasibility

core_structure.from: tokenization fit, semantic clarity, reusability, accessibility compliance, adoption feasibility
core_structure.to: motion token definitions, primitive library, migration plan, documentation, CI enforcement rules

stances: If an animation cannot be expressed as a token, question whether it should exist at all.
Never name a motion primitive after its visual effect. Always name it after what it communicates.
